rubric_item_scores.criterion_name: Remediation scenario modeling and budget feasibility
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include a short rationale in the memo for the recommended ordering (e.g., why O2 preferred under conservatism) and add a table showing cost breakdown per option to make auditing cost sums trivial.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All single- and multi-option scenarios are enumerated with multiplicative percent-change application to the correct target variables (temperature, duration, z-value). Costs are summed additively and compared correctly to the max_budget (132000); infeasible combinations are flagged. Scenario-level cumulative eq-min results and feasibility flags are internally consistent.

metadata_json.grading_notes: Model-judged grading performed using memo text, raw actual outputs, and scenario tables. All numerical checks (formula application, multiplicative percent changes, cumulative sums, cost feasibility checks) were recomputed or spot-checked and matched provided outputs.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Ensure the spreadsheet exposes visible formula cells for each per-record calculation (row-level formula cells) so a verifier can open the workbook and confirm formulas directly. I could not open the XLSX formulas programmatically (openpyxl unavailable) and relied on the memo and provided numeric outputs.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Per-record lethality values in the memo and raw_actual_outputs match the stated formula L = duration_min * 10^((temperature_C - 80) / z_value_C). Manual spot checks: R1, R2, R3 values recomputed and match the provided numbers. Percent-change remediation application is multiplicative and applied correctly in scenarios (e.g., O1 temp_factor=0.88 applied to each record; O2 duration_factor=0.82 applied to durations). Cumulative sums equal the sum of per-record lethality values shown.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Open the spreadsheet with a verifier tool to confirm formulas are not stored as static values and that row-level citation cells reference record IDs directly. If any key computation cells are values-only, add formula cells and a 'formula audit' sheet linking rows to the embedded packet.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present per actual outputs and artifact previews. The memo includes the formula, row-level citations for R1/R2/R3, per-record tables, scenario tables, sensitivity checks, and a recommendation table. The produced_deliverable_items list confirms both artifacts were created.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include a brief note in the memo clarifying that remediation options were applied globally to all records (or, if intended otherwise, provide per-record application details). Also include the explicit formula cells used to compute combined factors in the spreadsheet for rapid verification.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All single-option and multi-option remediation scenarios are enumerated with multiplicative application of percent-change factors to the appropriate target (temperature, duration, z). Costs are summed additively per combination and compared to the stated max_budget (132000). Infeasible combinations are flagged (total_cost > max_budget) and labeled no-go; feasible combinations are clearly identified. Scenario numerics in the memo match recomputed values in the raw outputs.

rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Conservative needs_review flags present where packet had no missing numeric inputs; verifier might expect an explicit confirmation that no inputs were missing.
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: If no embedded-packet fields are missing, add an explicit statement in the memo 'No missing embedded-packet inputs found; needs_review markers provided only for procedural ambiguities' or remove unnecessary needs_review flags. This will align the documentation more closely with the rubric expectation that an explicit confirmation be provided when no fields are missing.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: The memo enumerates assumptions, includes sensitivity checks (+/- cases), and documents 'needs_review' flags. The stated assumptions used in the computations match the task assumptions (multiplicative percent-change application and formula). However, the memo also marks several items as 'needs_review' (multiplicative interpretation and global remediation scope) despite the embedded packet containing explicit percent changes and no missing numeric inputs. This is not incorrect, but it is a conservative annotation that could be clarified.
